name: Black Hill / Cat's Back Ridge
terrain: Narrow ridge crest, grass, rocky crag
highlights: Spine-of-a-cat ridge rising straight out of Herefordshire — the English shoulder of the Black Mountains.
points_of_interest: Black Hill trig (640m); Offa's Dyke path continuation
viewpoints: Vale of Ewyas, Hay Bluff, Hatterrall ridge, Malverns eastwards
parking_start: Olchon Valley layby at Cat's Back end
hazards_notes: Narrow rocky ridge — not for vertigo sufferers
